name: Telos City
description: Telos is a purported subterranean city said to exist beneath Mount Shasta in California, a prominent location in New Age beliefs. Proponents believe Telos is inhabited by advanced Lemurians, descendants of the ancient civilization of Lemuria, who escaped its destruction by retreating underground. These Lemurians are often described as highly evolved beings with advanced technology, spiritual wisdom, and telepathic abilities, living in peace and harmony. The concept originated in the early 20th century, gaining traction through the writings of figures like Frederick Spencer Oliver (A Dweller on Two Planets) and later through the channeling work of Dianne Robbins, who claimed to communicate directly with Telos inhabitants. Evidence cited includes alleged psychic communications, personal experiences near Mount Shasta, and interpretations of geological anomalies. Mainstream science dismisses the existence of Telos and Lemurians as lacking empirical evidence. Geologists confirm the volcanic nature of Mount Shasta and find no evidence of large subterranean cities. Skeptics attribute the belief in Telos to pseudoscientific interpretations, misinterpretations of geological features, and the human tendency to seek meaning and explanations in the unknown, coupled with the romantic appeal of a hidden, utopian society.
subtitle: Subterranean Lemurian City under Mount Shasta

claims.claim: Telos is a real, inhabited city beneath Mount Shasta.
claims.evidence: Psychic channeling, personal experiences, interpretations of geological anomalies.
claims.criticism: Lack of verifiable physical evidence, reliance on subjective experiences, dismissal by mainstream geology.

claims.claim: Telos inhabitants are Lemurians, descendants of an advanced ancient civilization.
claims.criticism: Lemuria is not recognized by mainstream historians or archaeologists as a real civilization; channeled information is considered pseudoscience.

claims.claim: Telos inhabitants possess advanced technology and spiritual wisdom.
claims.criticism: No scientific evidence of advanced technology; spiritual wisdom is a subjective concept with varying interpretations.

keyFigures.name: Dianne Robbins
keyFigures.role: Channeler claiming contact with Telos inhabitants

keyFigures.name: Frederick Spencer Oliver
keyFigures.role: Early proponent of Lemurian mythos and subterranean civilizations

keyFigures.name: Geologists studying Mt. Shasta
keyFigures.role: Providing geological data contradicting the presence of large subterranean spaces
